title: Head of Saint John the Baptist
tombstone: Head of Saint John the Baptist, c. 1550-1650. Spain or Northern Italy, mid 16th - mid 17th century. Oil on canvas. The Cleveland Museum of Art, Mr. and Mrs. William H. Marlatt Fund 1953.424
culture: Spain or Northern Italy, mid 16th - mid 17th century
did_you_know: The subject was among the most reproduced devotional images of the Counter-Reformation.

title: Standing Apostle (Probably Saint John the Evangelist)
tombstone: Standing Apostle, c. 1430. Master of the Saint-Omer Apostles (Southern Netherlandish, active Bruges?). Alabaster. The Cleveland Museum of Art 2022.41.1

creators.description: Master of the Saint-Omer Apostles (Southern Netherlandish, active Bruges?)

description: Carved for a Flemish altarpiece ensemble.

title: Cuneiform Tablet
did_you_know: This receipt records deliveries of sheep and goats for temple offerings.
